Redesign the AgentDashboard cockpit (work in sidecar/web/: index.html, style.css, app.js) into a polished, cohesive interface for a human (the Chair) to review and navigate the agent organization. DESIGN BRIEF: the PRIMARY color is STEEL BLUE (around #4682b4) — build a harmonious, professional palette around it (steel-blue accents and headers, calm neutral surfaces, restrained status colors for agent states working/idle/blocked/error and for change states planned/approved/implemented). Keep ONE consistent theme throughout — same radius, spacing scale, type scale, and color tokens everywhere. Establish a clear visual hierarchy and a logical, scannable layout: a clean top header (brand, the current goal, cycle state, spend vs cap, and the PAUSE-ALL control), then well-grouped panels that are easy for a human to review at a glance — the ORG ('who is doing what now'), the BOARD (the kanban AND the change-management approvals, clearly sectioned), the ACTIVITY feed, and the agent CONSOLE. Improve spacing, alignment, typography, and consistency so it feels calm, modern, and trustworthy. Use the impeccable skill to guide every decision. domain=frontend. CRITICAL: preserve the live data wiring exactly — keep the element ids #agents #kanban #changes #feed #console #goal #cycleState #spend #killBtn #conn and the EventSource('/events') flow intact; restyle and reorganize, do not rewire. DONE WHEN: 'node --check sidecar/web/app.js' exits 0 AND sidecar/web/index.html still contains every one of these ids: agents, kanban, changes, feed, console, goal, cycleState, spend, killBtn.

Add an AGENT CHAT view to the cockpit so the Chair can watch the agents 'talking' to each other. Work in sidecar/web/ (and sidecar/src/server.js ONLY if you need to expose grouped data). Render the agents' recent activity as a readable team conversation: take the existing /api/state `console` lines (each is 'HH:MM:SS [agent-id] text') and present them as chat-style messages grouped/labeled by agent, with a distinct-but-harmonious bubble color per role (planner, developer/frontend-lead, tester, auditor, security), a timestamp, newest at the bottom, auto-scrolling. It should read like a Slack-style team channel. Match the steel-blue theme established in the redesign exactly — consistent tokens, no new palette. Use the impeccable skill. domain=frontend. CRITICAL: preserve all existing element ids and the EventSource('/events') flow; you may ADD new ids for the chat view but do not remove or rename existing ones. DONE WHEN: 'node --check sidecar/web/app.js' exits 0 AND sidecar/web/index.html still contains the ids agents, kanban, changes, feed, console.
